title: amaracarpus pubescens subsp. microphyllus
intro: Amaracarpus pubescens subsp. microphyllus, also known as the Microphyllous Amaracarpus, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Amaracarpus pubescens subsp. microphyllus include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions and decoctions, which are traditionally consumed to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions.
